meta.setting: Hard-rock mine tunnels, timber sets, powder smoke, ore cars, seep water, and shift crews under the Jefferson hills
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with mine danger, labor pressure, bad air, and close-quarters trouble

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining, settlement labor, and extractive pressure in the Whiskeytown region.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Gold-rush mining community context and period material culture.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Hard-Rock Tunnels region page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: H3 local marker line in the register of Timber Sets, Powder Smoke, and Dripping Stone
square image reference immediately after the marker line
opening blockquote built as three short generated lines: tunnel object, air or sound detail, plain table meaning
two compact paragraphs establishing tunnels as worksite, hazard maze, hiding place, evidence trail, and class-pressure chamber
closing blockquote that gives a generated tunnel sign and practical table meaning
generation_guidance: Generate hard-rock tunnels as practical work spaces: timber sets, ore cars, powder boxes, hand drills, candles, seep water, bad air, and men counting minutes to shift change.
Give the table scene starts around missing workers, unpaid wages, a blocked drift, stolen ore, unsafe bracing, hidden papers, or an animal spooked at the portal.
Keep the danger physical and playable; darkness should change decisions, footing, sound, smoke, and who can see whom.
Let labor politics and company neglect sit in the tunnel walls without turning the page into finished backstory.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: The tunnel should feel like it is listening through wet wood.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A hard-rock tunnel scene should ask what the crew heard, what the foreman concealed, and which timber is ready to complain.
must_preserve: Use 1899 western mining and labor language only.
The generated page must include image path regions/images/hard-rock-tunnels.png.
The prompt must not contain final prose, final blockquotes, or finished field marks.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not make the underground space mystical or heroic
do not use modern mine machinery, electric safety gear, plastic helmets, or clean industrial staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about 1899 hard-rock mining tunnels as hazards and scene engines.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded Jefferson State western play.
